title: Innodata (INOD) Q1 2026 Earnings Report
text: Innodata delivered strong first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations. Revenue climbed 54% year-over-year to $90.096 million, significantly outpacing analyst estimates of $76.469 million. The AI data engineering company reported adjusted earnings of 42 cents per share, substantially above the consensus expectation of 17 cents. Adjusted gross profit expanded to $42.6 million with margins reaching 47%, while adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to $25 million from $12.7 million in the prior year. CEO Jack Abuhoff announced an upward revision to full-year 2026 expectations, stating the company is increasing its revenue growth forecast to approximately 40% or more year-over-year, up from the previous guidance of approximately 35%. The company now projects full-year revenue of about $352.38 million, exceeding analyst estimates of $341.5 million. The company disclosed significant expansion with a major Big Tech customer expected to generate approximately $51 million in revenue this year. According to leadership, Twelve months ago, our revenue from this customer was zero; this year we expect it to become our second-largest customer. Innodata shares surged 43.73% in premarket trading Friday to $65.59 per share, reflecting investor enthusiasm over the strong results and improved outlook.
